entry.title: Retention GL
entry.content:
Hi SAP Gurus,

I have a client requirement to create a special GL to hold the customer retention amount for until the retention amount is due for receivable.

Can you please help that how can we create a special GL for customer retention Process.

entry.title: Re: Single User getting disconnected/session drop without any error or warning message
entry.content:
Hello Isaias,

can you please help me with below logs.
M *** WARNING => ThResFreeSession: free resources of T401_U22896_M0 (normal mode, complete free), rc=WARNING-SESSION-CANCEL_BY_SYSTEM, db_action=TH_DB_ROLLBACK [thxxmode.c 890]
M {root-id=005056B540051EDF9BD3FC4224DBC970}_{conn-id=00000000000000000000000000000000}_0
M ***LOG R47=> ThResFreeSession, delete () [thxxmode.c 1166]
M ThResFreeSession: delete T401_U22896_M0

where is the issue. users are getting disconnected continuously

Regards,
Priyanka

entry.title: Re: How to consume S/4HANA Cloud API using ABAP program from the same S/4HANA Cloud system
entry.content:
Hi @Gheorghe-Valer,

I apologize: I was too fast and did not read the entire question thoroughly enough, and I was caught by the question "Is there better alternative ... without having the need of putting in the logon credential in the program." So I was focusing on the question where to store user name and password in case of remote connectivity. I did not realize that the actual requirement is to call an API within the same system. In that case, the recommended approach is using the corresponding business object interface, of course. I will add another answer.

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: Re: Single User getting disconnected/session drop without any error or warning message
entry.content: Hello Isaias, can you please help me with below logs. M *** WARNING => ThResFreeSession: free resources of T401_U22896_M0 (normal mode, complete free), rc=WARNING-SESSION-CANCEL_BY_SYSTEM, db_action=TH_DB_ROLLBACK [thxxmode.c 890] M {root-id=005056B540051EDF9BD3FC4224DBC970}_{conn-id=00000000000000000000000000000000}_0 M ***LOG R47=> ThResFreeSession, delete () [thxxmode.c 1166] M ThResFreeSession: delete T401_U22896_M0 where is the issue. users are getting disconnected continuously Regards, Priyanka

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud EPIC Integration
entry.content: EPIC

entry.title: Re: Proposal for Product XXXX Plant XXXX MRP Area XXXX ca...
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu 

You should implement the note 3490869 - DDMRP: Descriptive error messages for buffer proposal rejections.

It doesn't solve the issue of buffer proposal adoption rejection but it provides more meaningful messages instead of a blanked PPH_DD061 Proposal for Product &1 Plant &2 MRP Area &3 can't be adopted error. Once the note is implemented you will get a more detailed message indicating what the problem really is.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: How to configure rebate in a way that the condition is visible on quotation level in SAP ECC?
entry.content:
Hi all, I have configured the rebate according to this source: Rebate Process Configuration - SAP Community

This has been done successfully, and the rebate is only shown at the billing level. Now, I would like the rebate condition to be displayed in the pricing scheme at the quotation level. How do I do this?

entry.title: Re: Purpose use of Offset account SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hello @nikunjmehta2290 
In SAP, offset accounts are used to: 

Match debits and credits: Ensure that each debit posting is matched to a credit posting, and vice versa, to comply with the law  

Clear items: Clear debit and credit items, assets and liabilities, and owner's equity  

Prevent profit and loss inflation: Collect amounts from accounts to be capitalized to prevent the profit and loss statement from inflating 

Offsetting is the process of removing or limiting liabilities by offsetting an entry with an equal but opposite entry. For example, a loss in one division can be offset by an equal profit in another division.

entry.title: Re: NEW GL Allocation Part 2
entry.content:
I have the same question. Can GL account be a receiver?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: FAIL: process hdbdaemon HDB Daemon not running
entry.content:
Hi Bhat,

Check this SAP note 2953430

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: ABAP Calling last calling program in the stack
entry.content: Normally the calling program continues after "call transaction". Maybe you have some additional code there which stops the program. Let us know your code around this call.

entry.title: Practical Insights and Experience Sharing in Developing Custom Tree Widgets for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Within the story of SAP Analytics Cloud, different visualizations are needed for different scenarios. SAP Analytics Cloud offers a wide range of charts and widgets to help users with data visualization, but some are not perfectly suited to the needs of a wide variety of practical scenarios. The custom widget framework for SAP Analytics Cloud allows to add your own custom widgets that provide a specific visualization of user. At this point, customers can customize their widgets by uploading custom widgets and use them in stories.

Based on the demo of the custom widget in the Help Portal, I developed a tree custom widget. This blog focuses on the process and references I use to implement a custom widget from scratch, as well as sharing practical experience during development.

Click here for the demo video.

This blog will elucidate the process of implementing custom widgets, detailing how to upload and utilize them in SAP Analytics Cloud, and will be organized into four main sections for a comprehensive overview. The outline of the blog is as follows: 

  • Introduction to Custom Widgets  
  • Custom Widget Development Process  
    • index.json  
    • main.js 
    • styling.js  
  • How to Upload Custom Widgets in SAP Analytics Cloud ？ 
  • Summary 

1. Introduction to Custom Widgets  

The SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget framework allows you to use your own custom widgets to extend the predefined set of widgets available in optimized stories. This can be useful, for example, if you need specific user interface elements, specific data visualizations, or specific features that aren’t available with a predefined set of widgets. Custom widgets can be seamlessly integrated into optimized stories for SAP Analytics Cloud. Like predefined widgets, custom widgets offer the following features: 

  • It is listed in the widget menu list from where you can add it to the canvas. 
  • It can be moved and resized on the canvas. 
  • It appears in the widget outline. 
  • It can contribute script methods to the analytics designer script language. 
  • It can provide areas in analytics designer where you can set property values of the custom widget at design time (Styling Panel and Builder Panel). 

 The source file of a custom widget consists of two types of files: a JSON file and resource files (JavaScript files).  

  • JSON File  

JSON file contains metadata in widgets, URL references to resource files, and so on.  

  • Resource Files  
    • main.js (the actual custom widget)  
    • styling.js (the Styling Panel of custom widget)  
    • builder.js (the Builder Panel of custom widget)  

Each of these JavaScript files is a web component. Make your own custom widget to start with the files above. 

2. Custom Widget Development Process  

In the tree custom widget I developed, mainly contains the three files “index.json”, “main.js”, “styling.js”. 

Index.json 

The following properties must be included in the JSON file of a custom widget: 

  • id 
  • version 
  • name 
  • newInstancePrefix 
  • webcomponents 
  • properties 
  • methods 
  • events 
  • dataBindings 

Here you have to guarantee the uniqueness of the id, which is your custom widget's unique ID. When you upload a custom widget that already exists, the system will remind you that if you proceed with the upload, it will overwrite the previously existing custom widget with the same id. 

A web component object specifies a custom widget within a web component. For each web component object, three properties are necessary: “kind”, “tag”, and “url". Kind can only be one of "main," "styling," or "builder," and the url is the relative position of the JavaScript file corresponding to the web component. 

Note: The tag is the unique identifier of the custom element of the web component whose name must contain at least one hyphen (-) to distinguish a custom element from a regular HTML element. And the tag must match the definition in the JavaScript file, or else the system will not be able to find the corresponding JavaScript file and will prompt the following error. 

Inside my custom widget, only the two types "main" and "styling" are used. If you also want to customize the content of the builder panel, you also need to add a web component of type "builder". 

The properties object contains some properties of a custom widget and their default values. Each property name in the properties object must correspond to the property name of the custom widget. For example, in the properties, you can define properties of the custom widget such as width, height, node color, font size, etc., which can be used in the JavaScript file. The values of the properties can be modified through the styling panel and then passed to the main component for re-rendering, thereby changing the style of the custom widget. 

The Methods object specifies the script methods included in a custom widget, including property setting methods. Each property name in the Methods object corresponds to a script method name in the custom widget. For example, in my widget, the setNodeFontSize method is defined to set the font size of a node. This method is used in main.js, and whenever the value of the nodeFontSize property changes, the function will retrieve the new value and update the corresponding font size in the component. 

By defining dataBindings in JSON file, you can access data from the model within a custom widget. The data returned by dataBindings mainly consists of two parts: 

  • data: This is an array containing all the returned data. Each object in the array represents a piece of data (the value of a measure corresponding to one or several "dimensions"). 
  • metadata: Metadata contains attribute information about some "measures" and "dimensions" present in the data. 

The format of the data returned by dataBindings may not necessarily match the format required by the custom widget. You can reorganize the structure of the data based on the data format requirements of a Echarts chart. For more information on using dataBindings, please click here . 

Examples of data returned by dataBindings are as follows. 

For a more detailed description and usage of the individual valid attributes in the JSON file, see the documentation on the Help Portal .   

main.js 

In the main.js file lies the core part of my custom widget. As I aimed to create a tree structure, I referenced the tree component in the Echarts library for display. After obtaining data through dataBindings, I wrote a function to transform the data structure into a tree structure. Having the correct data structure is the most crucial step for the successful display of the component. Once the data is correctly integrated into the Echarts tree component, a basic tree display structure can be obtained, which can then be optimized and adjusted according to individual needs. 

For instance, in my custom widget, I wanted the tree structure to support different orientations. This led to the issue of adjusting the direction of each node's label. Therefore, I needed to consider every distribution scenario of the tree structure and set the direction of label display accordingly. For a tree distributed from left to right, the labels of leaf nodes are displayed to the right of the node, while labels of other nodes are displayed to the left. In a tree distributed from top to bottom, the labels of leaf nodes are shown below the node, while labels of other nodes are displayed above. Similar adjustments apply to other distribution scenarios. By default, my node labels display the node name and its corresponding value. In cases where a node has no value, only the node name is displayed. Of course, you can customize the display content according to your requirements. 

The orthogonal layout effect is as follows: 

The radial layout effect is as follows: 

In the diagram below, you can see that the nodes in the tree structure are either hollow or solid. Hollow nodes indicate that the node cannot be further expanded, while solid nodes indicate that clicking on the node can expand its child nodes. 

When a threshold is set in the style settings panel, nodes in the tree diagram with values exceeding the threshold will be highlighted in red. This helps us effectively display which nodes in different hierarchical structures exceed the threshold.  
(Note: In my code, the default threshold value is set to 1,000,000,000. If you wish to modify this default value, you can edit the default value at line 233 in the sample code main.js, and also at line 79 in index.json.)   

A threshold value of 50,000,000 is set in the styling panel, and the nodes in the tree chart with values over 50,000,000 are highlighted in red.  

I chose to show the node name, value, and unit information inside the tooltip. 

If you want to customize the tooltip format, you can modify this part of main.js.

Here need to point out that currently this version of the tree widget only implements a version that supports one measure, and if you want to implement a tree that supports multiple measures, you can add multiple series to your code to present the values of multiple measures.  

styling.js 

The content in the styling.js file controls the module for additional properties of the custom widget in the style settings panel. 

In my custom widget, I aim to modify some basic properties of the tree component, change layout settings, and highlight nodes exceeding a threshold. Therefore, my style panel settings are as shown in the diagram below.  

Layout settings are divided into two types: orthogonal layout and radial layout. The orthogonal layout allows for setting the distribution direction as "L-R," "R-L," "T-B," and "B-T." 

It is important to note that only the orthogonal layout requires setting the direction and edge type. When selecting the radial layout, these two properties are not needed. To avoid unnecessary errors, I have set the selectors for direction and edge type to be hidden by default when the radial layout option is selected. 

Regarding node color settings, the current version of this custom widget only supports manually entering some color names in English or hexadecimal and RGB color values, such as "dark blue," "#00FF00," "RGB(0,0,255)," etc. If you wish to further enhance this feature, you can consider integrating a color picker plugin.  

3. How to Upload Custom Widgets in SAP Analytics Cloud 

You can upload and host these resource files in SAP Analytics Cloud to use custom widgets in a story. You need to upload the JSON file of the custom widget along with the corresponding resource files to SAP Analytics Cloud. 

Navigate to the story page, click on the custom widget, and select the add icon to upload the JSON file and its associated resource files separately.  

Note: When uploading resource files, make sure to compress them into a .zip file format and ensure that the .zip file is smaller than 10MB.  

In the story, add the custom widget by finding the widget you uploaded and inserting it into the story.  

Open the builder panel, select your model, and add "Measures" and "Dimensions." 

Note: The dimensions you add must have a hierarchical structure. When setting up different hierarchical structures, make sure to check "Include Parent Node Elements" to correctly display the tree structure. 

In the styling panel, you can modify and apply its properties in the Custom Widget Additional Properties module. The properties that can be modified in the styling panel are determined by your definitions in the styling.js resource file. In a tree custom widget, the styling panel can change the style of a tree, such as root node name, layout settings, direction settings, connection edge types, node types, and so on.

4. Summary 

The above is the complete process of developing a tree custom widget, which can be applied to various data visualization and analysis scenarios with hierarchical structures. 

For example, it can be used in: 

  • Organizational structure display in human resources: Show the hierarchical relationships among departments and positions within a company through a tree structure, clarify employee responsibilities, and assist management in optimizing human resource allocation and adjusting organizational structures. 
  • BOM display in production scenarios: Utilize a tree structure to display the bill of materials (BOM) of products and their hierarchical relationships, supporting production planning and material management to help companies control costs and improve production efficiency. 
  • Display of account hierarchy structure in financial statements: Use a tree structure to display detailed information on the general ledger, sub-accounts, various expenses, and income levels, helping financial personnel and management analyze financial conditions more intuitively and identify potential issues. 
  • Sales performance display for different regions, departments, or product lines: Use a tree structure to display sales performance from global revenue down to sales performance at the continent, country, and regional levels, helping companies identify which regions or product lines contribute the most, or discover sales bottlenecks. 

In the first version, there are still many incomplete aspects. If this tree diagram is helpful for you, you can download the source code  for reference and make modifications and adjustments based on it. 

During the implementation of the custom widget, I found some very helpful blogs. If you are interested in creating your own custom widget, you can refer to them:  

Use Custom Widgets (Optimized) | SAP Help Portal

Custom Widgets | SAP Help Portal

SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget Hands-on Guide - SAP Community

SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-On... - SAP Community

Your first SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget - Int... - SAP Community

Your first SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget – Par... - SAP Community

Your first SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget - Par... - SAP Community

Your first SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget – Par... - SAP Community

Your first SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget – Par... - SAP Community

Your first SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget – Par... - SAP Community

The above blog depicts my journey as a beginner learning and developing custom widgets, along with some insights gained along the way. I hope this blog proves to be helpful to you.  

 

This sample package is for learning and communication only, not for production use. It is critical that you check and thoroughly read the licenses of any third-party libraries that may be used in it before using it. By using the examples in this repository, you confirm that you understand and agree to abide by all the terms and conditions set forth in those licenses. Please use this resource pack responsibly and any use of this resource pack will be at your own risk.

entry.title: Re: How to consume S/4HANA Cloud API using ABAP program f...
entry.content:
Hi @yongkc,

you should use the business object interface in this use case.

From the error message I derive that the call violates the RAP BO implementation contract, that is, you call the save action during the interaction phase. See https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/action-definition#action-types: „Save actions are actions that can only be triggered during the RAP save sequence specifically during the FINALIZE or ADJUST_NUMBERS. Calling a save action defined as save(finalize) during the interaction phase like for example from a determination on modify results in a short dump.”

To solve this problem, you can buffer the "to be performed action" during the interaction phase and then perform the actual action (from the buffer) during the save sequence, for instance. 

For more information regarding RAP BO implementation contract, also have a look at 

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/general-rap-bo-implementation-contract
  • https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abapinvalid_stmts_in_rap_methods.htm

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello, @David_Chaviano 
Hope you are well 
my name is nourhan Tarek, and I'm a woman living in Egypt I would like to start a career in SAP, 
recently I took an interest in pursuing a career as an SAP consultant and I think this will be a great place to start. 
I'm interested in joining the digital skills initiative. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: QC result recording after UD in SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The solution is to set the Master Inspection Characteristic control indicator 'long-term inspection'.

See attached.

entry.title: Re: Time sheet alert doesn't go away
entry.content:
Hello Junwoo,

if you will not be able to get rid of the alert by recalculation/re-run of time sheet import, you should be also able to delete the alert itself in manage data.

You can use Integration center to quickly find the the respective external code. Or export obviously 🙂

Hope it helps.

BR

Dan

entry.title: Re: SAP DBTech JDBC: [7]: feature not supported: Unsupported parameter type:ST_GEOMETRY error while
entry.content: Error: feature not supported: [object Object] constant string is not allowed in Table type parameter:

entry.title: Re: QC result recording after UD in SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

We have activated the Long-Term Inspection for the MIC but when we are doing the result recording again for the same material the result recording screen is showing "No Characteristics were found". Can help me to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: In One Pan10liters of Oil is poured and the multiple...
entry.content: @Caetano Thank you for the answer, but my problem is that the amount of oil for each product cannot be calculated because it is a single frying pan and 10 liters are added to it. It remains in the frying pan for two days to fry the products, and on the third day it is thrown out of the frying pan and another 10 liters are added, and so on. (38-40 products are fried in this frying pan, and each product depends on its sales. It is possible that product number one is fried in this frying pan 11 times a day, depending on demand, and product number two is fried 17 times, and so on.)

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi...I am Happy to join the SAP community....

entry.title: Re: Hello experts! is there limitations in public cloud t...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

There is no relation between the Dynamic Modification Rule and automatic Usage Decisions.

In case the material is set to allow automatic Usage Decisions it would be possible to trigger workflow to take the automatic Usage Decisions, but it is also possible to schedule a job that takes the automatic Usage Decisions for 'SKIP lots and lot where all MIC have the completion status (5).

Programs to schedule:

- inspection type 01/04, (transaction QA10): program: RQEVAI30

- inspection type 03, (transaction QA40): program: RQEVAI50

 

\

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in category of the Following 

  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students

    About Me 
    Thrilled to pursue my passion for SAP consulting! Based in Cairo, Egypt, I hold a degree in construction management and have three years of experience as a Planning Engineer, focusing on project management and data analysis. My interest in ERP systems led me to explore SAP MM and SD modules, and I’m eager to apply my knowledge to real-world challenges. I believe my hands-on experience and dedication to learning make me a strong fit for the program. Excited to take this next step in my career

Merve

entry.title: Re: 4 Steps to getting SAP SuccessFactors Best Practices Content in your Customer Implementations
entry.content: We implemented the best practice packages in the development system. Considering that the client accepts precisely the functionalities from the best practice and the customization intervention was minimal, how can we transport the functionalities to production?

entry.title: Re: set_lowercase( ) from cl_salv_column_table not working
entry.content:
Hi, I tried it and I agree it is confusing.
The purpose of this flag is obviously now what we expect?

Here Łukasz explained "set_lowercase" little bit in his blog + example:
https://abapblog.com/component/content/article?id=172:alv-grid-in-the-nutshell-field-catalog-lowercase-lowercase-letters-allowed-not-allowed

So to set fields to lowercase/uppercase you need to update data in internal table yourself. Before you display them in ALV.

entry.title: Re: SAC Odata Service Connection Failed
entry.content:
I've waited a long time without receiving a solution. After some investigation, I discovered that we need to use the OData metadata URL to connect with SAC.

I was initially using the OData service URL for a specific entity, which did not allow me to connect. However, when I switched to the metadata URL, it started working for me.

entry.title: shopping cart in MM for SAP Help
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

How are you?

I have some questions about the shopping cart in MM for SAP, I need to know and information about shopping cart and what, where, How? ..... etc, for where is using the shopping cart in SAP and how to use this in any project.

And what is the benefit for shopping cart and what is the circle or workflow for the shopping cart for any project if you have any example?

If there anyone you can explain to me and if you have any website or photo to help me pls shared with me.

Thank you,

#ERP #MM #SAP #material_management #MM #cart #s4hana #sap #help #support #workflow

entry.title: Re: Deleting Developer in HANA Studo and object ownerships.
entry.content: Hi, Did you get solution to above problem listed, If there are some best practices it is good to know.

entry.title: CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF hex value 0d not printed in adobe form QR code
entry.content:
Hello.

Our Invoice form has a QR code and CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF is concatenated to the barcode string to trigger a carriage return and line feed in between the barcode fields.

On debug mode CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF shows hexadecimal value 0D000A00 and it is still present in the concatenated string (hex value = 0033003100300D000A00390032003), but when the QR code printed on the PDF form is scanned, only 0A00 or 0a is captured (attached screenshot of scanned value and converted to hex value).  I tried to separate CR and LF by concatenating CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF(1) and CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF+1(1), scan result shows hex value 0a 0a.   Barcode was scanned using a barcode tester software (where we get the converted hex value) and by RF scanner (expected result 2 blank lines in between values but actual result is just 1 blank line in between). 

We have a legacy form printed from a non-SAP application with the same QR code, the scanned hex value has 0d 0a, this is from the same barcode test software.

I hope somebody can help me on this. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud V2: object text or textfield
entry.content:
Hello,

I need help. I am trying to create a custom field that can be used as a text editor (my customer is storing their texts for Sales Quotes). 

Could you please help me to choose the correct extensible field? I tried object text but this is not allowing me to write. Also, I need to resize the field for multiple lines. 

Thanks for any suggestions. 
Jakub

Dear expect,
I face some cost issue in Sub contracting movement, can you explain the step-by-step process along with movement cost values

entry.title: Re: Need Help with Transportation Costs & Margin Calculat...
entry.content: Answer : Set Cost Component Structure for Transfer

entry.title: Re: Before AP Invoice Created Inventory out from SAP Business One
entry.content: Could you please explain it properly

entry.title: Re: How to Get Sap menu by RFC or Bapi or Programe
entry.content:
By program? (Manually, there's a download for favorites.)

For favorites. search the Web around SMEN_BUFFC or BX_FAVOS_READ_ALL_NODES.

For "SAP Menu", it's the same for all users. The users can also display another menu which is role-based (varies for every user). For "SAP Menu", it's a menu named S000, use STREE_STRUCTURE_READ_N_TOP_LEV. As I don't find answers in the Web, I post the code here:

  DATA structure_id    TYPE ttree-id.
  DATA read_all_levels TYPE hier_types-char1.
  DATA read_n_levels   TYPE hier_types-numc2.
  DATA structure_table TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF hier_iface.

  structure_id = 'S000'.
  read_all_levels = 'X'.
  CALL FUNCTION 'STREE_STRUCTURE_READ_N_TOP_LEV'
    EXPORTING
      structure_id          = structure_id
      read_all_levels       = read_all_levels
      read_n_levels         = read_n_levels
    TABLES
      structure_table       = structure_table.

entry.title: Is it possible not to display cleared items on ICMR?
entry.content:
Hello gurus

Is it possible not to display cleared items on Intercompany Matching and Reconciliation?

I just want to know open AR/AP only.

 

Thank you in advance

SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting

entry.title: I want to create an iFlow to register vendor invoice.
entry.content:
I want to create an iFlow to register vendor invoice.
The simplest case with one line of records to read and one credit and one debit account was successful.
However, we would like to support complex cases. For example, there are multiple accounts. For example, a journal entry using 63001000 and 63002000 for G/L Account on the debit side and 21100000 for the credit side. And there are multiple tax rates, each of which is calculated.

Example:

Example of records to be read:

There is one header line, one line for 63001000 and one line for 63002000.

I am having trouble creating an iFlow for such a case. I'm looking for samples or articles that might give me some hints, so if you know of any, please let me know.

entry.title: Unable to save nested handling units
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

As part of the goods shipping process, we would like to display the details of the handling units in the packing list as provided in the shipment. Packaging instructions have been created to enable the creation of nested handling units.

To initiate the handling unit creation process, I use the following application :

Once the information is entered, we can see the desired hierarchy based on the packaging instruction. (See image below)

 

After clicking on "Create and save handling unit," we receive confirmation that the handling units have been created and saved. However, after this save, I am unable to retrieve all the information, whether in the inventory or the packing list.

Is there a missing step in the handling unit creation process ?

Could you please assist me with this ?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA XS Advanced Application - Node.js service Fr...
entry.content: Thanks for reply. I will test it.

entry.title: Re: FM READ_TEXT is not showing the data but the SAP standard transaction ME33L does show all the da
entry.content:
After Raymond has posted the right solution, it makes me think that a universal solution is to use the SQL trace (ST05) to understand quickly (in minutes) what's going on.

In your case, you would see right after steps 1 or 2 (ME31L or ME32L) that the access to STXH/STXL concerns the material master data, and you would also see the values to access STXH/STXL.

entry.title: SAP Revolution Journey from ECC to S/4 HANA
entry.content:
SAP Revolution Journey from ECC to S/4 HANA

 Introduction: Hey guys, hope you all are doing good. Today, I am here to talk about SAP ERP software. I recently started learning this software with objective to work and build my career in this technology as I came to know that this business ERP is world most popular and highly adoptable by big companies of various industries like- Amazon, Google, big Automobile companies, Steel manufacturing companies, various service company like Banks, IT companies like Accenture, Tech-Mahindra, E&Y, Oil & Gas industries, Production companies etc.

I am a beginner and have a great interest to learn or deep dive into it and thus keep searching for good explanatory blogs on the same by our SAP senior consultant which I got but in a very scatter manner so with thought to provide relevant SAP and its generation revolution information in a very crispy manner which helps many of us like me who wanted to start their career.

Before I start, I heartfully thankful to our mentor Mr. Vikram Fotani who has given me opportunity to learn and grow.

What is SAP- It is called “System Application and Product in Data Processing”. It is an enterprise application software which helps Industries or Companies to run and record their businesses operation end to end on daily basis of any size and types of business operations. It is highly successful ERP software system that includes all the tools and processes which required to run business successfully for any industries or companies which covers Manufacturing Resource, Production Planning, Financial Management & Accounting, CRM, Human Resources, Customer Services, Payments and many more.

It has been a great and successful history, let me give you a small glance of it.

  • It has been founded in year of 1972 in Walldorf, Germany by five IBM’s engineers. It has started with R/2 in 1979 and then came up with R/3 in 1992 which used to call three tier architecture consisting of the database, the application server, and the client later then the most popular product launched in 2004 which was called ERP which was populated with name ECC (ECC stands for SAP ERP Central Component, which is an enterprise resource planning (ERP) system that's part of the SAP Business Suite. It's one of SAP legacy applications. It is an integrated software solution and highly successful product.
  • Further there was a revolution when SAP launched its 4th generation product in 2015 SAP S/4 HANA which is in-memory database platform, it’s an intelligent enterprise resource planning software which can be deployed On-Premises or On Cloud or Hybrid. It has unique feature of real-time data processing and analytics and that makes it very advance revolutionary upgrade to ERP of SAP. Here, S stands for Simple and 4 stands for fourth generation.
  • Year by year SAP have upgraded the S/4 HANA products based on customer feedback and keep on releasing its new updated version which are more uploaded with new feature from its previous version along with fixing of inconsistency in particular product and bugs. Now today we are having SAP S/4 HANA 2023 (108).

SAP S/4 HANA Deployment Options:

SAP S/4 HANA On Premise- In this common model, the customer acquires a license and installs the software in their own data center. It has all industries business solution, extendable, modifiable and yearly innovation get released.

SAP S/4 HANA on Cloud (Public Cloud)- In this model, the customer rents the software for a certain period of time. SAP deploys the software in the SAP-owned data center. Public cloud software typically runs in a multi-tenant server, where multiple tenants, or customers share the resources of the server.

SAP S/4 HANA on Cloud (Private Cloud)- Private cloud software typically runs in a single tenant server, where only a single tenant (customer) uses the resources of the server. Software runs in a private network protected by a firewall, similar to an on-premise system.

The main differences between private cloud and on premise is who has responsibility for maintaining the server, and the license for the software installed on the server.

SAP S/4 HANA Hybrid- In this model, required applications are operated partially by the customer and partially by SAP in the Cloud. Both parts can be linked and integrated with each other.

SAP S/4 HANA Mobile- In this model, mobile devices access On Premise, or Cloud applications.

There is other digital model which can be explained here but that will not much help us being beginner that is why I don’t elaborate them now but in future in my other blog I will cover them as well.

Now, since I have given plenty of information on the ERP software and its history, we all also start thinking how this works in real world for any industry or company. Well, as of now or say till now what I knew is that to implement SAP S/4 HANA ERP for any company SAP has provide a road map and particular methodology to follow step by step and achieve the goal of using this highly useful and adoptable business software. Let’s know about it on high level.

SAP ERP Implementation Methodology-

SAP has provided three methodology and the decision to use or adopt by company as per their need.

What are all those three methodologies-

  1. ASAP Methodology- The Accelerated SAP (ASAP) methodology is a project management framework used to implement SAP solutions. It is designed to help companies streamline the implementation process, reduce risks, and ensure that SAP projects are completed efficiently.

The ASAP methodology consists of five phases:

Phase1-Project Preparation- In this phase we discuss the project, identify resources, identify project objective, Build a qualified and capable project team.

 

Phase2- Business Blueprint- This phase is very important phase as we discuss with business on various business process, Gather the business requirements, prepare the As is and As on.

 

Phase3-Realization- Prepare the system as per the Blue Print, Test the system, Business User do the testing, Training done for Business users.

 

Phase4-Final Preparation- We prepare and ready the system for Go- Live, Legacy data load activity.

 

Phase5-Go-Live and Post Go-live support- In this phase SAP system goes live where business user start doing business transaction live into system and SAP consultant helps in resolving for any issue or error.

 

  1. SAP Activate Methodology- The primary purpose of the SAP Activate Methodology is to provide end-to-end Application Lifecycle Management and to accelerate the initial implementation of SAP S/4HANA. SAP Activate Methodology phases: D-P-E-R-D-R Discover, Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy and Run
  2. Agile Methodology- Agile methodology is very updated one and many organizations are now a days adapting to it. SAP Agile Methodology is a way to manage SAP projects by applying agile principles and best practices to specific organizational needs. This methodology helps to make SAP solutions more efficient, responsive, and flexible.

I am very new to AGILE and have no experience and therefore I prefer not much speak on this topic but in future I will release blog on this topic as well.

 

I have tried to present here in very short journey of learning as much as possible and I will keep releasing in future based on my learning and experience new topics in this space. Thanks to all, please like and subscribe and share feedback to improve. Happy Learning to all.

# SAP S/4 HANA FICO @ SAP Community

entry.title: New SAP SuccessFactors Certifications for Certified Individuals in SAP HCM (C_THR12) Available
entry.content:
The shift to cloud computing has undoubtedly changed the way organizations carry out their HR operations. For SAP users, the cloud-based future of Human Capital Management lies in the SAP SuccessFactors solution.

That is also why the maintenance period for SAP Business Suite 7 (ECC) and the previous SAP ERP HCM system as an on-premise solution ends on December 31, 2027. If you are already operating the SAP HCM solution in the Compatibility Pack (CP) with SAP S/4HANA, the usage rights will end on December 31, 2025. As a result, the SAP Certification SAP Certified Associate - SAP HCM for S/4HANA (C_THR12) will be discontinued on December 31, 2024. You can find the planned retirement date on the certification website. Certified users will stay certified for 12 months after the certification retirement. The final due date will be visible on My Learning Certifications (sap.com). Please also note that the academies THR10 and THR12 a– the trainings to prepare for the certification, will be discontinued by the end of 2024. 

But don’t worry about the retirement! To ensure you stay on top of your HR game, SAP stepped up and continues to launch future-focused earning and enablement resources for you, making sure you excel in a cloud-first HR world. On the SAP Learning site, you can find tailored, bite-sized learning and certification opportunities for the SAP SuccessFactors solution, including topics like recruiting, workforce analytics and planning, talent management, payroll, and more. A few highlights include: 

Learning journeys for beginners: 

  • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core 
  • Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors Platform 

 SAP Certifications:

  • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Compensation 

To learn more about certification retirements, check out this FAQ. To learn more about the ways to get and stay certified, visit this FAQ. Browse all available SAP Certifications here.

entry.title: Re: Retrieving the FCM Token on iOS
entry.content: Sorry, have no experience with iOS at all.

entry.title: Re: CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF hex value 0d not printed in adobe form QR code
entry.content: Generally speaking, 0D0A has the same meaning as 0A, both mean ONE "carriage return", 0D0A is used by Windows systems, 0A is used by Linux systems (Newline - Wikipedia). The scanners may propose some settings concerning the interpretation of Newline.

entry.title: Re: Latest Identity and Authentication Management features introduced with SAP Business One FP 2305
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

how can I copy SSO user mappings from LIVE SAPB1 server to TEST SAPB1 server?

the customer has ~30 SAP databases and ~100 SAP users...

ps. I'm not asking about copying user mappings between the databases on users tab on SLD, but between the SAPB1 servers

entry.title: Archiving question
entry.content: I got a question on archiving. If we are archiving application data for SD - SD_VBAK, SD_VBRK etc....and if the data being archived has attachments(external attachments and BRF+ outputs), even if we select "archive attachments" the actual attachment that is stored in DB/Content Server/Filesystem/External storage like Sharepoint/AzureBlob is not actually getting deleted. I mean data is getting archived and data from output is getting convered to RAW data, data is getting deleted from SAP tables and archived to whichever location we set it up as.  Is this normal in SAP that SAP standard archiving doesn't actually delete the attachments/outputs created thus leaving them as zombies because the link information in DMS is deleted so one cannot know which business object this attachment belongs to. Can you throw some light on how you managed this situation. This way all attachments are left as zombies for all the data that is archived and this seems like a big issue. I don't know how else to analyse this further

entry.title: Re: Is it possible not to display cleared items on ICMR?
entry.content:
Hi @miyoungpark,

Yes, it's possible to exclude Cleared items in ICMR matching and reconciliation:

  • To exclude them from document matching based on rules, use filter "Clearing Status <3" in data slices in matching rule definition (app Define Matching Methods).
  • To exclude them from displaying in reconciliation reports (app Reconciliation Status Overview/Reconciliation Balances), set the similar filters in display groups of reconciliation case (app Define Reconciliation Cases)

It's recommended to not set the filter at the matching method level (method-level filters), because the method filters determine the data scope, which should be keep stable instead of changing dynamically. See FAQ note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3113859 for more info.

Hope it helps. Thanks!

Grace

entry.title: Re: SAP extends ERP cloud portfolio with "SAP ERP, private edition, option for Microsoft SQL&qu
entry.content:
This is a brilliant move by SAP.

They offer a two-stage approach to moving to RISE/GROW with SAP. The first step is to migrate your systems to the cloud and familiarize yourself with the different operating model in which SAP operates your systems. Once you are familiar with this, you can migrate to RISE/GROW.

This basically makes the S/4 transformation (if RISE or GROW is the target architecture) less risky and splits the big bang transformation into two sub-projects: A pure IT project (move to the cloud) and an S/4 business transformation project.

And it goes without saying that the revenue counts as cloud revenue already during the ERP operating phase.

entry.title: Re: Cannot deploy Fiori Element app to S/4 HANA Cloud Pub...

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Floor Price Setting
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, S/4HANA Cloud can definitely meet your business objectives. Your business requirements regarding sales price management in the quotation process are quite common.

Here's a summary of your business expectations:

  • **Headquarter Company Level Pricing**: A unified sales list price (net) will be defined for each product by material ID.
  • **Customer-Specific Discounts**: Each customer may have a unique discount percentage based on the unified company list price (net).
  • **Regional Floor Pricing**: Each sales region will have a floor price (net amount) by material ID and sales organization (or other condition fields) relative to the company list price (net).
  • **Quotation Restrictions**: Sales reps are not allowed to save a sales quotation if the net price, after all discounts (including customer-specific and manually input bidding discounts), falls below the predefined sales region floor price.

To implement this logic and process, you'll generally need three key aspects:

  • **Customized Sales Pricing Procedure**: This will reflect and calculate the sales list price, customer discount, bidding discount, net price after discounts, and the sales region floor price.
  • **Price Elements Access Control**: This ensures different business roles have appropriate visibility of the price conditions on sales documents.
  • **In-App or DEV Extensions**: These will raise an error message to block the quotation from follow-up processing if the net price after discounts is below the predefined sales region floor price.

To customize the pricing procedure, you might need to configure several condition types. You have the option to use pre-delivered, out-of-the-box condition types or create custom ones:

  • **Unified Sales List Price**: An example is PPR0, which is quantity-based. Its value is automatically determined using an access sequence.
  • **Pre-defined Customer Discount**: An example is DC01, which calculates the discount amount using a pre-defined percentage rate and multiplying the condition value of PPR0 by defining "from-to" steps at the pricing procedure level.
  • **Manually Input Bidding Discount**: An example is DRV1, with its value manually entered by the sales representative rather than being auto-determined from an access sequence. For the condition type setup of manual input price elements, ensure that the "Access Sequence" field is empty and "Manual Entries" are not set to "B" or "D". You can maintain flexibility in the "Calculation Type" by choosing between "B Fixed Amount" and "C Quantity".
  • **Sales Region Floor Price**: This configuration is similar to the sales list price but should be a statistical value only and not included in the net price after discounts in the pricing procedure.

For more information on price elements access control, you can explore the reference info collected in 3405405 - Role-Based Access to Price and Cost Details in SD Documents - S/4HANA Cloud (sap.com).

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content:
thank you the assistance, but I am having some issues, as I am using abap 7.20

APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_comp ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_outcomp>).

wont work, nor will any use of VALUE or DATA. to fetch dynamic structures/types. So <fs_outcomp>  would need to be delclared with some sort of structure I presume, as it otherwise would not be able to say   <fs_outcomp>-name,

 

also I am uncertain about  lv_fieldstr = data ELEMENT. Perhabs this is also a version issue, but what element would this refer to ?

I have the following problem: Some of my clients in production are configured in SCC4 as test clients. I want to change them to productive clients. But every time I want to save the change, it just changes back to test.

I don't have access to the 000 client as it is a Hana Private Cloud and access to 000 is not given to anyone. 

I also tied to set the system change option to changeable and then change the client role, without success.

Do you know a way I can do that?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?

entry.title: How to manage odd quantities in Packing BoM
entry.content:
We are manufacturing individual unit as Make to Stock and we release production order from SAP for 100 qty to produce. We have design the system to keep the SFG BoM and FG BoM Seperate.

Now the question is, we are storing 31 Units under 1 pallet and if we divide the 1000 then 32.25 pallets are required. Now 32 pallets will have 31 Units and each unit attached with the serial number as well AND 32nd Unit will only have 8 Units.

How we can manage the situation wherein we can track the pallet-wise qty and serial number inside each pallet with the use of standard SAP.

Our packing will be done prior to it goes to the storage unit.

Can anyone assist us with good SAP Practise to manage the same.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?

entry.title: Solman completed Job Alert Creation
entry.content:
We are trying to set up email notifications for successfully completed SAP batch jobs. Currently, we are only receiving notifications for failure cases.

Could you please guide us on how to configure the metrics for successful job notifications?

Thank you for your help!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solman completed Job Alert Creation
entry.content:
There is no metric available to send alerts when job runs successfully. Alerts are sent only when yellow or red status occurs.

Nevertheless, there is a way for Job Monitoring to send alerts based on job logs text:

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/solution-manager/expert-portal/job-monitoring.html?anchorId=section_1563786446

Review configuration guide from Configuration Guide Job Monitoring 7.2  --> 5.8.11 Job Log Content (ABAP Job and BW Process Chain Element)

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content:
ABAP 7.02 (7.20 is an obsolete name, or one for Kernel or SAP GUI):

 

FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_outcomp> TYPE ANY.
APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_comp ASSIGNING <fs_outcomp>.

entry.title: Re: SAP CDS view move from NONDESNOTCONNECTED to a new Ca...
entry.content:
Hello @wjdsouza 

I know that you can assign your development package, where your CDS views are assigned to, to an Application component (in SE80).

At least for SAP Datasphere you will find all CDS Views under component: BW-DWC

I assume if you take one, which exists in your replicated tree in RSA1, then they should also occur there?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: How to use Data Store Object (DSO) in ABAP CDS?
entry.content: An external view can be generated out of the DSO. Information can be found here: https://www.nextlytics.com/blog/external-hana-views-how-to-generate-external-hana-views-from-bw-objects

Let us know any time you have questions!

entry.content:

In the last week, I can't see my project.

I created an automation project, but in the last week, when I click on my project in the SAP build lobby I am getting a blank page, where before I used to get the list of the components of my projects.

It is the same, no matter what project I am trying to open.

I tried my trial account and it is the same, and my colleague are also having the same experience.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Handle PDF response from called URL using sendRequest...
entry.content:
Hi Lim,

Thanks for your reply. As my requirement is to get the PDF as BLOB, I would refer to your suggestion

"If you need the PDF response in Blob format:
You can also use the Fetch API on the iOS platform to download a resource and handle the response as a Blob, similar to how it’s done in web environments."
 
I would like to inform you that I have already tried this rather this was my first approach but the Fetch API specifically in this case does not work correctly on iOS platform specifically. The execution stops after the fetch call on iOS but the same thing works on Web. I cannot analyse/debug the Fetch call as the same is not recorded by Mobile Network Trace, only sendRequest is recorded.
So, kindly guide me if the Fetch for this simple GET needs to be called in a different way for iOS or is there a way to do sendRequest and get the response as BLOB.
 
Looking forward for your valuable inputs.
 
Best Regards,
Geet

entry.title: Replication Flow : Tables not getting imported using ABAP connection
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are currently using a free trial of Datasphere and attempting to create a replication flow with an ECC (on-prem) system via an ABAP connection. The connection through the cloud connector works well, and we can see all the objects under the ODP_SAPI container.

However, when selecting any source object (In last step : "Add source objects") , the import fails without showing any error (please see the attached screenshot). I am wondering if anyone else has encountered this issue while working with the replication flow, or if there is something we might have missed during the configuration process.

Looking forward to your feedback.

 

Best Regards,
Yatendra Soni

entry.title: SAP S4 HANA Vs EC Payroll
entry.content:
Hello Experts

Currently we are using On Premise SAP ECC HCM, Time Mgt, Payroll  FICO, MM, SD, PP etc and planning to move to S4 HANA. We are also using SuccessFactors Employee central and other Talent modules (Time and payroll are still in On premise SAP ECC).

I want to know what is the best approach. Should we go for EC Payroll or should we go for S4 HANA payroll. Is there are pros/cons Matrix available for the same

Please advise

Thanks and Advance

entry.title: Error locking E070_ASYNC_PROC
entry.content:
Hi All,

Sometimes when you try to release a TR from charm, and if the developer has the relevant object open in EDIT mode in development system, charm is unable to release the TR and throws below mentioned error in the charm change document application log. 

Error locking E070_ASYNC_PROC

I swear I remember navigating to the details of this error somewhere in SAP, to accurately identify which user has locked which particular object, that is stopping the TR release. But I've forgotten the flow/steps how to view that error. 

Would someone be able to help with the steps how and where can I see this error please. The error details help pinpoint the user as well as the precise object being locked.

entry.title: FBRA Error: 'Document XXXXXXXXX is not purely a clearing document,
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to request your assistance with SAP. Could you guide me on how to reverse a clearing document that includes an allocation as a partial payment to a customer account?

Document details for reference:

Encountering error in both reversal tcodes:

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Uploaded .PDF file by DTW in OCRD Table in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi @labindia123 

Your attachment's record should exist in the OATC and ATC1 Table first. You can import attachment's record via DTW (Administration - System Initialization - Attachments2).

Once you have all the attachments in OATC and ATC1 Table, you need to use ‘AtcEntry’ column in the template for OCRD.

‘AtcEntry’ column in OCRD Template should have the value of ‘AbsEntry’ column from the OATC Table for a particular attachment.

entry.title: Re: Sub contracting process
entry.content:
Hi,

Refer to these links

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/subcontracting-process-in-production-planning-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13578805

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/subsequent-adjustment-in-sub-contracting/ba-p/13556358

Regards

entry.title: Re: Error connecting to system using ADT on eclipse
entry.content: 1) I have opened SMGW and tried to logon using Eclipse and I see the connection on the transaction.

entry.title: Re: How to disable Excel Auto open in SAP GUI
entry.content:
Hi,

Coordinate with BASIS consultant and configure security rules here

 

Here is SAP Document for your reference.

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_windows/ca5169c2f72448eeb608cd09564ccf90/54636fcfd3b144e3bc770c27e1e14079.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/tips-stop-the-pop-up-sap-gui-security-quot-remeber-my-decision-quot/ba-p/13302177

Regards

Regards

entry.title: How to maitain Additional Data in CL01
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I can not find "Additional Data" tab in CL01 while create class.  

Actually there is a parameter "CLASSADDITIONAL" (ddic type:BAPI1003_ADD) in the FM "BAPI_CLASS_READ".

So definitely it's possible to maintain Additional Data for Class in somewhere.

Anybody can can give some advice to maintain Additional Data in CL01?

Or how to enable "Additional Data" tab in the CL01?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Error connecting to system using ADT on eclipse
entry.content: 2) In any other case we can connect to the system and we can telnet the port. 3) I don't think is the problem because I tried on annother pc with updated ADT and it didn't work but it could connecct ot the system via gui

entry.title: Re: Error connecting to system using ADT on eclipse
entry.content: Yes, and I can access it without any problem using the gui

entry.title: Process Type in Purchase Order web service request
entry.content:
How to flag the process type (e.g. to-stock or non-stock or third-party) in the standard web service for purchase order(service name: ManagePurchaseOrderIn) in Business By Design system?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Process Type in Purchase Order web service request
entry.content:
Hi,

In the purchase order standard web service request, we do not have specifically "process type" field.

But there is a Direct Material Indicator.

This indicator indicates if the item is a stock or consumable item. If the flag is set, then it is a stock item.

So, if you want to specify the process type , you can set the indicator as True or False :

 <DirectMaterialIndicator>false</DirectMaterialIndicator>

 <ThirdPartyDealIndicator>true</ThirdPartyDealIndicator>

Best Regards,

Ishita
